text: Iran War Live: Day 30 overnight — IDF launched 'widespread wave' of strikes on heart of Tehran: missile production facilities, command centers, naval weapons HQ all hit. Explosions heard across city; blasts in northern Tehran. Iran retaliates with cluster munitions over Tel Aviv — 8 impact sites, 1 civilian killed (60M), 2 injured. War enters its second month at maximum intensity on all fronts.
verificationNote: IDF Tehran strikes confirmed by Times of Israel Day 30 liveblog and Al Jazeera. Cluster munitions and Tel Aviv casualty confirmed by CNN and Times of Israel.

text: Conflict Monitor: Islamabad Day 2 begins with a tangible deliverable: Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships (2/day) through Hormuz. This is the first crack in the blockade. Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar confirmed the deal. It restores <0.5% of pre-war Hormuz flow but is symbolically significant — Iran signaling to international mediators that it can offer something. Germany's FM: direct US-Iran meeting 'very soon.' April 6 deadline looms.
verificationNote: Pakistani-flagged ship arrangement confirmed by Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar. German FM statement per Al Arabiya. Confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 30 liveblog.

text: ISW Day 30 assessment: At one month, Iran has fired 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones since Feb 28 — ~40% at Israel, ~60% at US targets. US can confirm only ~1/3 of Iran's missile arsenal destroyed; status of another 1/3 unknown. Iran may be launching small salvos throughout the day deliberately to keep Israeli civilians in perpetual shelter. 85% of Iran's surface-to-air missiles reportedly destroyed, exposing Iranian airspace to continued US/Israeli strikes.
verificationNote: ISW Critical Threats March 27 assessment. 85% SAM destruction per Israeli Army Radio cited by ISW. Missile/drone totals per ISW tracking of IRGC Aerospace Force waves.

text: Energy Intelligence Pro: Day 30 economics — Brent settled at $107.81/bbl as of Friday March 27; WTI $99.64/bbl. AAA national gas average hit $3.98/gal — up $1.00 in exactly one month (pre-war $2.98/AAA). Goldman Sachs estimates $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium currently baked in. Dubai physical crude trading at $126/bbl in spot markets. Trump's April 6 Hormuz deadline is the key price catalyst. If Iran holds, $130+ Brent scenario becomes consensus.
verificationNote: Brent/WTI prices per Fortune/CNBC March 27. AAA gas average per AAA Newsroom March 26. Goldman risk premium from published research. Dubai crude per Bloomberg physical markets.

text: Houthi Monitor: After firing two missiles at Israel on Day 29 (one ballistic, one cruise), Houthis have signaled they are in the war for the long term. Key risk: they have not yet executed on Bab al-Mandeb closure threat. If they act on that, combined Hormuz+Bab al-Mandeb blockade would be the worst peacetime maritime crisis since Suez 1956. 35% of global shipped goods would be disrupted simultaneously.
verificationNote: Second Houthi missile (cruise) confirmed by CNBC and Al Jazeera. Bab al-Mandeb threat per Houthi deputy minister. Trade volume figures from 2024 global shipping data.

text: Aurora Intelligence: Day 30 key data points as war enters month 2 — Iran 1,963+ killed, 24,910+ injured; Lebanon 1,189+ killed; US 13 KIA, 315+ WIA; Israel 28 killed. IDF conducted 8,500+ strikes in Iran; 400 Iranian ballistic missiles destroyed; 335 missile launchers eliminated. IRGC threat against US universities in Gulf (Texas A&M Qatar, Northwestern Qatar, NYU Abu Dhabi) is a new psychological escalation. IDF Sgt. Moshe Katz (22, New Haven CT) killed in south Lebanon — 7th IDF soldier killed in Lebanon since war began.
verificationNote: Death tolls from Al Jazeera tracker and Lebanese Health Ministry. IDF strike counts from IDF spokesman. University threat per NBC News liveblog. Katz death confirmed by Times of Israel Day 30 liveblog.

text: OSINTdefender: Open-source imagery from March 29 confirms total destruction of E-3G AWACS tail 81-0005 at Prince Sultan Air Base — fuselage split in two, rotodome displaced. This is the first US strategic command-and-control aircraft destroyed in combat since the Cold War. Also confirmed: 1 KC-135R completely destroyed, 3 heavily damaged. Iran's ability to hit aircraft at hardened NATO-grade facilities is a major intelligence success for the IRGC.
verificationNote: Imagery confirmed by Air & Space Forces Magazine and The War Zone. Tail number 81-0005 identified by aviation OSINT analysts.

text: Military Analysis Network: Pentagon ground ops planning for Iran (WaPo) is the most significant escalation signal since Day 1. Key data: Kharg Island handles ~80% of Iran's oil exports. A US seizure would be decisive economically but tactically complex — island is well-defended, shallow coastal waters favor Iranian mines. 82nd Airborne is the right tool for rapid-insertion raids but would need massive air cover. Iran's Parliament Speaker's response (threatening to 'fire and punish') signals they've read the same WaPo article.
verificationNote: WaPo ground ops report confirmed. Ghalibaf statement confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog. Kharg Island strategic importance per EIA petroleum data.

text: Gulf Security Intel: IRGC's targeting of Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) and EGA Abu Dhabi is strategically significant — both supply aluminium to Boeing, Lockheed, and BAE Systems supply chains. IRGC is specifically trying to create 'dual-use infrastructure' targeting precedents to justify civilian-sector strikes. Alba is 69.7% owned by the Bahraini government. EGA is 50% owned by the Abu Dhabi government. These are not random industrial targets — they are state-owned assets with defense sector exposure.
verificationNote: IRGC claim confirmed by Al Jazeera and CNBC. Alba and EGA ownership structure is public record per company filings.

text: Pakistan-Iran Diplomacy Monitor: Day 30 update on Islamabad summit — Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan, Turkish FM Hakan Fidan, and Egyptian FM Badr Abdelatty all present. Pakistan FM Dar confirmed 20 Pakistani-flagged ships approved for Hormuz transit (2/day). Two ships already crossed: MV Multan and P-Akili crude tanker (80M+ litres) en route to Karachi, docking March 31. This is operationally significant — the blockade's first actual crack. Iran appears to be using ship permissions as diplomatic currency ahead of any direct US-Iran meeting.
verificationNote: Summit confirmed by Al Jazeera. Ship names and cargo per Pakistan Today and Argus Media. Docking date per Pakistan Today.

text: Iran War Casualties Monitor: Updated Day 30 casualty summary — Iran confirmed killed: 1,963+ (Iran Health Ministry, Day 28); HRANA estimate: 3,202+ (Day 23). Lebanon: 1,189+ confirmed killed, 2,740+ wounded. Israel: 28 confirmed killed (7 IDF Lebanon + 21 civilians). US: 13 KIA, 315+ WIA. Gulf states: 27 confirmed, 282+ wounded (adding 4 from March 29 Bahrain/UAE aluminium attacks). France: 1 KIA, 7 WIA. Iraq: 83 PMF killed. Total confirmed war dead (all sides): 1,300+ in 30 days.
verificationNote: Figures compiled from Al Jazeera tracker, Lebanese Health Ministry, IDF, CENTCOM, HRANA, Lebanese Red Cross, and regional government reports.

text: Conflict Intelligence Network: Day 30 threat assessment — three converging developments raise escalation risk sharply: (1) Pentagon Kharg Island ground ops planning now public; (2) IRGC threatening US universities in Gulf with noon March 30 deadline; (3) Houthi missiles now reaching central Israel with debris casualties. Simultaneously, (4) Islamabad summit is the most structured ceasefire process yet; (5) IDF says days from completing all priority targets. Classic endgame pressure-diplomacy dynamic: maximum military threat meeting maximum diplomatic opportunity. Trump April 6 Hormuz deadline is the forcing function.
verificationNote: All component facts confirmed through Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, WaPo, and NBC News liveblogs.

text: Chemical Hazard Watch: Iranian missile fragment hit ADAMA (Syngenta) Makhteshim plant in Ne'ot Hovav — facility produces organophosphate-based pesticides; Home Front Command issued shelter-in-place for Ne'ot Hovav zone; 3 hazmat teams deployed; Environment Minister Silman issued all-clear after atmospheric monitoring found no dispersal; 11 injured in Beersheba from main missile strike.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel confirmed the ADAMA plant hit and Environment Ministry all-clear.

text: Persian Gulf Naval Monitor: US-Israeli strike at Bandar Khamir port (Hormozgan Province) — 5 killed, 4 injured per IRNA; port quay hit along with 2 vessels and 1 vehicle; Bandar Khamir is 25km from Strait of Hormuz and primary IRGC small-boat staging facility; strike continues pattern of systematically dismantling Iran's maritime capability in the Gulf.
verificationNote: Al Bawaba citing IRNA confirmed the strike; Al Jazeera Day 30 liveblog corroborated.

text: Vatican Diplomatic Watch: Pope Leo XIV's Palm Sunday homily marks the 12th Vatican peace appeal since Feb 28 — this time with direct theological framing: 'Jesus, King of Peace, whom no one can use to justify war.' Most direct statement yet; frames both US-Israeli offensive and Iranian retaliation as contrary to Christian faith; aired globally via Vatican Media.
verificationNote: Confirmed by NPR and Vatican News from Palm Sunday Mass, St. Peter's Square, March 29, 2026.

text: Iran Missile & Production Monitor: IDF Lt. Col. Nadav Shoshani stated Iran 'currently does not have the ability to manufacture any new missiles' — refers to destruction of Parchin facility and other ballistic missile production sites near Tehran; IDF says '60 IAF jets dropped 150+ bombs' on facilities in/near Tehran in last 48h; this claim has significant strategic implications but remains an IDF assertion — independent verification impossible with Iran's internet blackout.
verificationNote: IDF spokesperson's claim per Times of Israel. Satellite data would be needed to fully confirm extent of Parchin destruction. Treating as likely pending independent assessment.

text: Iran War Live: Day 30 afternoon situation (11:15 AM PDT) — (1) Iran hits Beersheba + ADAMA chemical plant (11 injured, hazmat scare, all-clear issued); (2) US-Israel kills 5 at Bandar Khamir port near Hormuz; (3) Pope Leo Palm Sunday peace appeal (12th since Feb 28); (4) Araghchi tells Turkish FM: no real negotiations with US; (5) IDF: days from completing all critical Iran military-industrial targets; cumulative: Iran 1,968+ killed, Lebanon 1,238+ killed.
verificationNote: Compiled from Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, NPR, and CNN Day 30 liveblogs.

text: OSINT Technical: IDF evening Tehran wave notable for targeting Alborz province high-voltage transmission infrastructure — confirms IDF now treating Iran's civilian power grid as legitimate military target given grid powers missile production facilities. Power outages across 4 Tehran districts. IAEA confirmation of Khondab IR-40 destruction is milestone — second major nuclear facility confirmed destroyed after Fordow.
verificationNote: Power outages confirmed by Iranian state media and JPost liveblog. IAEA confirmation at 20:28 UTC confirmed by Times of Israel and Turkiye Today.

text: Intel Crab: UNIFIL peacekeeper killed near Adchit Al-Qusayr is strategically significant — Indonesia has the largest UNIFIL contingent (~1,200 troops) and Jakarta has been a vocal opponent of Israel's campaign in Lebanon. UNIFIL statement language ('may amount to war crimes') unusually strong. Watch for Indonesian political response and potential UNIFIL restructuring.
verificationNote: UNIFIL statement confirmed by RTE, Times of Israel, ANI News. Indonesia battalion confirmed by UNIFIL.

text: GeoConfirmed: WaPo missile infrastructure assessment confirms geolocated data — satellite imagery reviewed shows 29 of ~30 identified Iranian ballistic missile launch sites hit, ~330 of 470 launchers destroyed or damaged. IDF claim that Iran 'cannot manufacture new missiles' appears supported by Parchin and Khojir facility destruction. Remaining Iranian missile capability is pre-positioned stockpile, not production capacity.
verificationNote: WaPo investigation with satellite imagery review published March 29. IDF Lt. Col. Shoshani statement on Parchin confirmed by Times of Israel.

text: Diplomatic Monitor: Islamabad quadrilateral summit outcome assessment — Pakistan's mediation role is genuine and significant. FM Dar confirmed both sides have 'expressed confidence in Pakistan.' Trump reposting Dar's Hormuz announcement on Truth Social is notable signaling. Iran allowing Pakistani ships while refusing others suggests selective diplomacy. Key test: whether Rubio and Araghchi physically meet in Islamabad in next 48-72 hours.
verificationNote: Summit outcomes confirmed by Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, NPR. Pakistan 20-ship deal confirmed. Trump repost confirmed by Dawn and Pakistan Today.

text: Nuclear Security Monitor: Iran's NPT withdrawal legislation moving through parliament is the single most dangerous diplomatic development of Day 30. Unlike tactical military escalations, exiting the NPT removes IAEA inspection rights, eliminates legal constraints on weapons development, and creates a precedent for North Korea-style nuclear breakout. If passed, triggers mandatory UN Security Council emergency session and would likely be met with unprecedented US-EU-Israel response.
verificationNote: NPT withdrawal legislation confirmed by Al Jazeera, Tasnim, Press TV, Middle East Monitor.

text: Iran War Live: Day 30 evening summary (7:12 PM PDT) — (1) IDF evening Tehran wave: 120+ bombs, power blackouts across 4 districts, 2 killed near Shaft; (2) IAEA confirms Khondab IR-40 destroyed; (3) UNIFIL peacekeeper killed, Adchit Al-Qusayr; (4) Houthi 2nd wave: 2 drones intercepted; (5) Trump: Iran agreed to 'most of' 15 points, Kharg Island still on table; (6) Islamabad summit: Pakistan ready to host Rubio-Araghchi talks; (7) Iran advancing NPT withdrawal legislation; cumulative: Iran 1,972+ killed, Lebanon 1,239+ killed.
verificationNote: Compiled from Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, CNN, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, NPR Day 30 liveblogs.

text: Iran War Live: Day 31 opening (5:27 AM PDT) — (1) IDF dropped 120+ bombs overnight on Tehran missile R&D sites, power blackouts in 4 districts, 2 killed near Shaft; (2) Tabriz Petrochemical Company struck at midnight Tehran time, fire contained, CEO confirms live on state TV; (3) Mehrabad Airport re-struck; (4) Iranian/Hezbollah barrage ignites fire at Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery (7th Iranian wave of night); (5) Indian worker killed in Iranian drone strike on Kuwait power/desalination plant; (6) IDF Alpinist Unit conducted first documented cross-border raid from Syrian Hermon into south Lebanon.
verificationNote: Compiled from Times of Israel, Anadolu Agency, Al Jazeera, Jerusalem Post, Kuwait Times Day 31 coverage.

text: Diplomacy Watch: Trump's Day 31 FT interview contains the most internally contradictory diplomatic messaging of the conflict: (A) claims 'regime change achieved' and Iran agreed to 'most of' 15 US demands; (B) simultaneously threatens to seize Kharg Island and 'obliterate' Iran's oil infrastructure. Iran publicly denied accepting any US demands. Analysis: classic Trump simultaneous pressure/olive-branch messaging. The actual forcing function is April 6 Hormuz deadline. Rubio-Araghchi meeting in Islamabad reportedly still on track for 'coming days.'
verificationNote: Trump FT interview confirmed by Bloomberg, NBC News, Time Magazine. Iran denial confirmed by ANI News citing Araghchi statements. Pakistan FM Dar's statement on Rubio-Araghchi talks per Al Jazeera.

text: NATO Affairs Monitor: Spain's formal airspace closure to US military planes in the Iran war is the most significant intra-NATO rupture since the conflict began. Three developments in sequence: (1) Spain barred use of Rota and Morón bases; (2) US relocated 15 aircraft; (3) Spain now closed all Spanish airspace to US planes in the campaign. Trump threatening trade war with Spain escalates this into an economic confrontation with a NATO ally. This creates a precedent — other NATO members watching closely.
verificationNote: Spain airspace closure confirmed by Spanish Defense Ministry statement, Euronews. Trump trade war threat confirmed by Times of Israel and NBC News.

text: Oil Market Intelligence: Day 31 oil surge to $114.90 Brent driven by three simultaneous catalysts: (1) Tabriz Petrochemical Company strike (energy infrastructure hit); (2) Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery fire (signal Iran will target Israeli energy too); (3) Trump Kharg Island seizure threat (market pricing in risk of even greater supply disruption). Goldman raised Brent forecast to avg $110 in March/April. Saudi oil officials cited by Bloomberg warn prices could exceed $180/bbl if disruptions persist through late April.
verificationNote: Brent $114.90 per CNBC March 30. Tabriz and Haifa strikes per Anadolu Agency and Jerusalem Post. Trump Kharg threat per Bloomberg and FT. Goldman forecast per CNBC.

text: Special Operations Monitor: IDF Alpinist Unit's cross-border operation from Syrian Hermon to south Lebanon is operationally significant. First time IDF publicly documented such a multi-domain cross-border maneuver. The operation reveals two things: (1) IDF is now treating the Syrian Hermon foothills as a forward staging area for Lebanon operations; (2) Hezbollah has maintained weapons caches in the Mount Dov buffer zone even under the Lebanon ground invasion. Bunkers and artillery found suggest Hezbollah prepared long-term stay-behind capability.
verificationNote: IDF Alpinist operation confirmed by IDF spokesperson, Times of Israel liveblog, Jerusalem Post, and JNS.

text: Aurora Intelligence: Day 31 escalation scorecard — (1) IDF strikes now targeting civilian economic infrastructure (Tabriz Petrochemical, Mehrabad Airport, power grid) alongside military targets; (2) Iran now striking partner-nation infrastructure (Kuwait power plant — 1 killed); (3) Trump threatening seizure of Kharg Island goes beyond any stated US war objective; (4) Spain-US rift is first direct NATO fracture. If all four continue simultaneously, Day 32-35 will see the war either substantially escalate or the Islamabad diplomacy process become urgent necessity.
verificationNote: All component facts confirmed by primary sources: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Euronews, Times of Israel Day 31 coverage.
